dataset.description: Tooth segmentation from panoramic X-ray images
dataset.acquisition_info: Panoramic X-ray images acquired using HiRes3D-Plus device (Changzhou Boneng Zhongding Medical Technology), resolution standardized to 640 × 320 pixels
dataset.annotation_info: Mixed annotation method: initial manual annotation by 20 trained dental practitioners using EISeg and LabelMe software, followed by iterative semi-automatic annotation using R2 U-Net predictions with manual correction and verification by 6 experienced dentists
dataset.data_source: Multi-center study from Hangzhou Dental Hospital, Hangzhou Qiantang Dental Hospital, and Sichuan Provincial People's Hospital
dataset.detailed_description: This dataset is a subset of the STS-2D-Tooth (Adult Panoramic X-ray Images) component of the STS-Tooth multimodal dental dataset. It consists of 850 annotated panoramic X-ray images (PXI) with corresponding binary segmentation masks for tooth segmentation. The images were acquired from adult patients using HiRes3D-Plus devices and were preprocessed to a standardized resolution of 640 × 320 pixels. The segmentation task focuses on delineating all tooth structures within the oral cavity, which is clinically relevant for dental diagnosis and treatment planning.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate tooth segmentation from panoramic X-rays assists dentists in detecting dental issues such as impacted teeth, skeletal anomalies, and cysts, and provides essential information for treatment planning and computer-aided diagnosis in dental care.
